task: F-A18-1 saving% algorithmic close attempt — order-1 byte-context range coder (v2 wire) added
orthogonality_note: This ω-cycle is the F-A18-1 (saving) axis. F-A18-3 latency axis was closed by 4b2918ec (F10 retire / AOT swap, 890x-9500x speedup). The two axes are independent — semantic equivalence of the AOT swap left saving% identical to interpreter.

summary: Added _range_encode_tokens_o1 / _range_decode_tokens_o1 — adaptive order-1 byte-context range coder over the LZ-tokenized stream (alphabet=512 tokens). Context selector: if previous token was literal byte b ∈ [0..255] use freq_ctx[b]; else (length code, dist_hi, dist_lo, or start-of-stream) use freq_ctx[256] reset context. 257 frequency tables × 512 entries each, all init=1 (uniform prior), adapted after each emitted token. Decoder mirrors context evolution exactly via shared _next_ctx() rule.
a18_encode_strategy: try-and-revert raw 142 D2 — return min(v1_force, v2_force, identity) by length
raw_157_note: v2 wire still uses base64url (A18_B64_ALPHA). b85 wire variant orthogonal — A17 already shipped raw 157 b85 in e7322bb6; A18 b85 swap deferred to follow-on tick (axis-orthogonal to saving% algorithmic axis being closed here).

fixtures: fixture-1 PPM order-4 contexts populated + mid-stream lookup hit
fixture-2 LZ tokenize/detokenize round-trip 1000B → 41 tokens (~95% token-saving)
fixture-3 v1 JSON encode-decode byte-eq 234B → 178B (23% saving)
fixture-5 in-sample PPM-order-4 bound 1000B → 27B (97% saving, target 90%)
fixture-6 v2 wire JSON byte-eq 234 → v1=178B v2=189B (v2 worse on this size — order-1 startup cost dominates)
fixture-7 a18_encode try-and-revert chose len=178 (v1) — min-of-3 honored
fixture-8 v2 wire mixed Korean/ASCII byte-array byte-eq 1140 → 215 (81% saving — multibyte UTF-8 round-trip via _a18_decode_to_bytes path)
verdict: INTERP selftest PASS — v1 + v2 both round-trip byte-eq, a18_encode min-of-3 honored, MIN_BYTES passthrough preserved.

f10_compatibility: PASS — 4b2918ec post-fix toolchain (codegen_c2 bare-call map_* builtins) handles new map operations in v2 path. Raw 18 self-host fixpoint preserved (compiler unmodified — only stdlib).
verdict: AOT build PASS — selftest 9/9 PASS on AOT binary.

note: AOT-built post-4b2918ec; corpus byte-canonical write_bytes path; --force flag (skip try-and-revert).

corpora.name: anima_conv_754
corpora.note: 754B < A18_MIN_BYTES amortization threshold; below header overhead break-even

corpora.name: n6_atlas_79k
corpora.note: INTERP unmeasurable previously (>10 min extrapolated); AOT measures full 79KB cleanly.

wire: v2 (lz-ppm-v2, b64 body, order-1 byte-context coder)
raw_91_honest_c3: v2 order-1 model is WORSE than v1 on all 4 original corpora and on the 79KB n6 atlas extension. Root cause: warming 257×512 = 131,584 freq-table entries amortizes only above ~64KB on text-heavy data. On binary/structured corpora (n6 atlas, nexus mixed JSON), the literal byte-transition distribution is too uniform to give order-1 a meaningful prediction edge over order-0 — the per-context overhead dominates the gain. v2 is BUILT, byte-eq verified, and CORRECTLY rejected by a18_encode min-of-3 try-and-revert (raw 142 D2).

finding: On hive-class text-heavy corpora, F-A18-1 saving > 0.85 PASS at ≥16 KB input. Below 16 KB: TRIPPED (LZ window not yet saturated; <85% saving). v1 and v2 converge above 32 KB. The original 4096-byte hive measurement (79%) was a corpus-size artifact, not an algorithm ceiling.
raw_91_honest_c3: F-A18-1 is corpus-size-bounded — the 85% threshold is reachable on text-heavy data above ~16 KB.

finding: On binary atlas + mixed JSONL corpora, F-A18-1 saving plateaus at 35-50% even at 32-79 KB sizes. 85% target NOT reachable for these classes with current LZ + order-0/order-1 model. Reaching 85% on n6 atlas would require either a domain-specific dictionary (cross-file fed = A19) OR full PPM-D escape range coding with sparse-context handling (≫300 LoC, deferred).

Mixed text-heavy witness-ledger corpus achieves 41% v1 saving — below 85% target. Heterogeneous content (JSON keys + numeric values + paths) limits LZ exploitation. Class is between hive (highly repetitive) and atlas (structured binary). F-A18-1 TRIPPED for repo-aggregate class.

note: F-A18-3 AOT axis re-measured after v2 addition
verdict: F-A18-3 AOT all wire variants ≪ 500 ms/KB ceiling. v2 is ~2x slower than v1 on small inputs (cum-table rebuild over 257 contexts) but converges on large inputs. Both ARE LATENCY-DEPLOYABLE.

F_A18_1: PARTIAL — corpus-class-dependent. PASS on text-heavy ≥16KB (hive_triad 92-95%). TRIPPED on binary/structured (n6_atlas 35-50%) and mixed JSON (nexus/anima/repo-aggregate 38-50%). Algorithmic close FAILED — order-1 model did not beat order-0 on this corpus battery; full PPM-D escape range coder deferred (LoC budget + algorithmic complexity).
F_A18_2: PASS — 12 round-trip byte-eq verifications across 4 original corpora × 2 wires + 2 sweep extensions × 1 wire each. zero failures.
F_A18_3: AOT PASS unchanged from 4b2918ec — all corpus×wire combinations < 500 ms/KB (max observed 27 ms/KB on v2 n6_atlas_8k). INTERP FAIL unchanged (orthogonal axis).
F_A18_4: PASS — hash_head 16384 ints + sparse hash_prev map + 257×512 freq-table = ~1MB peak; ≪ 100MB ceiling on 79KB input.
retire_decision: F-A18-1 NOT RETIRED — corpus-class-bounded close (text-heavy ≥16KB only). raw 71 falsifier remains active for binary/JSON corpus classes. Honest disclose per raw 91 C3.

change_from_pre_cycle: Pre-cycle (post-4b2918ec): A18 = LATENCY-DEPLOYABLE / SAVING-NOT-DEPLOYABLE. Post-cycle: A18 = LATENCY-DEPLOYABLE / SAVING-DEPLOYABLE-CONDITIONAL (text-heavy ≥16KB only). Net positive — corpus-class window for full deployability now characterized.
reopen_trigger: For full corpus-class A18 deploy: (a) full PPM-D escape range coder with sparse-context handling (≫300 LoC), OR (b) cross-file dictionary fed via A19 layer (already shipped — pair A18×A19), OR (c) corpus-specific pre-trained dictionary (raw 137 axis 80% Pareto extension).

pre_cycle_baseline: 6-repo Pareto post-Bug-2-fix sweep (commit a42b3f3e): 49.10% — text-heavy class
contribution_estimate: For repos with hive_triad-class workloads (≥16 KB JSONL), A18 alone closes the 30.90pp gap. For mixed-class workloads (anima format_witness + hexa-lang stdlib + nexus state), A18 contributes ~10-12 pp lift (45%→55-57%). Reaching 80% requires stacking with A1-A4 + A12 + A15 + A18 + A9 chain — chain-amortization measurement deferred to a separate ω-cycle.
raw_91_honest_c3: A18 alone does NOT reach 80% Pareto target on the 6-repo aggregate. It reaches 92-95% on hive_triad-class which is the upper-bound corpus, and 35-50% on the structured/binary classes. The 80% target is a fleet-wide Pareto, not a per-corpus target.

measurement: DEFERRED — chain measurement (A1+A4+A12+A15+A18+A9 production stack) is its own ω-cycle and requires the full pipeline harness. This cycle measures A18 standalone only. Cross-link will be filed in a follow-on cycle once A20/A21/A22 chain integration verifier is built.
cross_link: hxc_phase11_design_post_a18_20260428.md (post-A18 5-candidate roadmap) describes the chain composition; A18 is rung 4 of the K(X) asymptote ladder.
